signals.headline: Bank of Korea holds rate at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea holds rate at 2.5% despite inflation pressures.** South Korea's annual inflation rate rose to **2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in the previous two months, exceeding the central bank's 2% target.[1] This uptick was driven largely by surging oil costs stemming from the Middle East conflict, with import prices marking their sharpest jump in almost three decades.[4][5]
